result.markdown:
---
title: "Quarto dashboards — a clinical cockpit from code"
description: "Value boxes, a live plot and a sortable table arranged into a responsive dashboard — declared with format: dashboard."
image: thumbnail.svg
image-alt: "Quarto dashboards — a clinical cockpit from code — Blood Doctor · Quarto"
date: "2026-07-11"
categories: [Visualisation, Interactive]
---

Sometimes the output is not a paper but a **dashboard** — headline numbers, a
chart and a table, laid out as a cockpit. Quarto builds these from the same
Markdown + code you already know, with `format: dashboard`.

## Layout by headings, content by code

````yaml
---
title: "Anticoagulation Service Dashboard"
format: dashboard
---
````
````markdown
## Row {height=20%}

::: {#c72fe07e .cell content='valuebox' title='Patients in range' execution_count=1}
``` {.python .cell-code}
dict(value="78%", icon="heart-pulse", color="success")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
```
{'value': '78%', 'icon': 'heart-pulse', 'color': 'success'}
```
:::
:::


## Row

::: {#723111b0 .cell execution_count=2}
``` {.python .cell-code}
# a matplotlib chart fills this card
```
:::


::: {#16e15180 .cell execution_count=3}
``` {.python .cell-code}
# a pandas table fills the next card
```
:::


````

Rows and columns come from heading levels; `#| content: valuebox` turns a cell
into a KPI tile. The result is responsive and self-contained — no dashboard
framework, no server.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Open the live dashboard
See the [**anticoagulation dashboard**](/outputs/dashboard.html) — value boxes, a
computed chart and a data table, all from one `.qmd`.
:::
